Name: Effortless Banking
Description: Community-bank commercial RM platform — loans, deposits, covenants, BSA/AML.

Users.Description: Bank employees: relationship managers, underwriters, branch bankers, and admins. Used for portfolio assignment, audit trails, and segregation-of-duties enforcement.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased compound primary key derived from FullName.

schema.name: FullName
schema.Description: Display label of the bank employee.

schema.name: Role
schema.Description: Role discriminator: RM, Underwriter, BranchBanker, or Admin.

schema.name: Email
schema.Description: Bank email used for SSO and notifications.

schema.name: IsRM
schema.Description: True when Role = 'RM' (relationship manager).

schema.name: IsUnderwriter
schema.Description: True when Role = 'Underwriter'.

schema.name: IsBranchBanker
schema.Description: True when Role = 'BranchBanker'.

schema.name: IsAdmin
schema.Description: True when Role = 'Admin'.

schema.name: CountOfPortfolioBusinesses
schema.Description: Number of Businesses whose RelationshipManager is this user.

schema.name: CountOfOriginatedLoans
schema.Description: Number of Loans this user originated as the RM of record.

schema.name: CountOfUnderwrittenLoans
schema.Description: Number of Loans this user underwrote.

Businesses.Description: Small-business customers (and prospects) of the bank. Central entity: BeneficialOwners, Contacts, Accounts, Loans, Documents, and Interactions all hang off a Business. BusinessProfile information (legal name, structure, NAICS code) lives directly on this table.
Businesses.summary_rich: The **borrower file**. Each row is one small-business customer or prospect; everything regulated about that relationship — the BeneficialOwners on the CDD file, the Accounts and their balances, the Loans and their grades, the Interactions audit log — hangs off a Business via FK. `MeetsCddRule` enforces FinCEN's 25%-or-control-person rule; `PortfolioPriority` rolls up loan classification and CDD state into the single bucket the RM sorts the book by every morning.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased compound PK derived from LegalName (punctuation stripped).

schema.name: LegalName
schema.Description: Registered legal name of the business.

schema.name: BusinessStructure
schema.Description: Entity structure: LLC, C-Corp, S-Corp, Partnership, Sole Proprietor, etc.

schema.name: NaicsCode
schema.Description: North American Industry Classification System code for the business.

schema.name: NaicsDescription
schema.Description: Human-readable label for the NAICS code.

schema.name: AnnualRevenueUsd
schema.Description: Most-recent annual revenue, USD.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Lifecycle status: Prospect, Customer, or Closed.

schema.name: OnboardedAt
schema.Description: Date onboarding completed (KYB cleared, first account opened).

schema.name: RelationshipManager
schema.Description: FK to Users: the RM who owns this business relationship (the SinglePointOfContact).

schema.name: ReferralSource
schema.Description: Optional FK to another Business that referred this prospect/customer in.

schema.name: RelationshipManagerLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the assigned RM, pulled from Users.

schema.name: IsCustomer
schema.Description: True when Status = 'Customer'.

schema.name: IsProspect
schema.Description: True when Status = 'Prospect'.

schema.name: WasReferred
schema.Description: True when this business was referred in by another business.

schema.name: CountOfBeneficialOwners
schema.Description: Number of BeneficialOwner rows attached.

schema.name: CountOfContacts
schema.Description: Number of Contact rows attached.

schema.name: CountOfAccounts
schema.Description: Number of Account rows attached.

schema.name: CountOfLoans
schema.Description: Number of Loan rows attached.

schema.name: CountOfInteractions
schema.Description: Number of Interaction rows attached (the activity feed).

schema.name: CountOfDocuments
schema.Description: Number of Documents attached directly to this Business.

schema.name: TotalDepositBalanceUsd
schema.Description: Sum of CurrentBalanceUsd across this Business's Accounts.

schema.name: TotalLoanPrincipalUsd
schema.Description: Sum of PrincipalUsd across this Business's Loans.

schema.name: CountOfClassifiedLoans
schema.Description: Number of Loans on this Business whose risk grade is substandard or worse (>=7).

schema.name: HasClassifiedLoan
schema.Description: True when at least one loan on this Business is a classified asset.

schema.name: BeneficialOwnersAtCddThreshold
schema.Description: Count of BeneficialOwners meeting FinCEN's 25% threshold or marked as the control person.

schema.name: MeetsCddRule
schema.Description: True when CDD beneficial-ownership collection is satisfied: at least one owner crosses the 25% threshold, or a control person is designated.
schema.explanation_rich: **FinCEN's CDD rule, encoded once.** Every legal entity opening an account has to have either an owner crossing the 25% threshold or a designated control person on file. This field is a rollup from `BeneficialOwners` filtered by `MeetsCddThreshold` (the owner-level OR of `Meets25PercentThreshold` and `IsControlPerson`). Worked example: *Stillwater Roasters LLC* has Jordan Park (60%, also control person) and Sam Rivera (40%) — so `BeneficialOwnersAtCddThreshold` = 2 and the rule clears. *Hilltop Bakery LLC* is a Prospect with no owners attached yet — the rollup is 0 and `MeetsCddRule` is FALSE until the file is built. Postgres, Python, and Excel all agree, because there is only one definition.

schema.name: PortfolioPriority
schema.Description: Higher-order: portfolio-management priority bucket for this business. 'High' when classified loan present, 'Medium' when CDD or onboarding incomplete or no loans yet, otherwise 'Low'.
schema.explanation_rich: **The single bucket the RM sorts the morning call list by.** PortfolioPriority is a higher-order calc that consumes other calcs: it depends on `HasClassifiedLoan` (which depends on `CountOfClassifiedLoans`, an aggregation over `Loans.IsClassifiedAsset`), on `MeetsCddRule`, on `IsProspect`, and on `CountOfLoans`. Worked example: *Pine Hardware Inc.* owns L-0042 (RiskRating=7 Substandard, an `IsClassifiedAsset`), so `HasClassifiedLoan` = TRUE and PortfolioPriority resolves to **'High'**. *Hilltop Bakery LLC* is a Prospect with no loans yet and no CDD file — it resolves to **'Medium'**. *Stillwater Roasters LLC* is a Customer with a clean grade-4 loan and a clean CDD file — it resolves to **'Low'**. Downgrade L-0042 back to grade 4, and Pine drops from 'High' to 'Low' the same instant every substrate recomputes.

data.Name: sunset-auto-body-llc
data.NaicsDescription: Automotive Body, Paint, and Interior Repair

BeneficialOwners.Description: Individuals owning 25%+ of a Business plus designated control persons, per FinCEN's CDD rule. PII (SSN, DOB, address) is stored encrypted at rest via field-level encryption.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased compound PK: {Business}-{FullName slug}.

schema.name: Business
schema.Description: FK to the parent Business.

schema.name: FullName
schema.Description: Owner's full legal name.

schema.name: DateOfBirthEncrypted
schema.Description: Encrypted date of birth (PII, FieldLevelEncryption).

schema.name: SsnEncrypted
schema.Description: Encrypted Social Security Number (PII, FieldLevelEncryption).

schema.name: AddressEncrypted
schema.Description: Encrypted home address (PII, FieldLevelEncryption).

schema.name: OwnershipPercentage
schema.Description: Percent of the Business owned by this individual (0-100).

schema.name: IsControlPerson
schema.Description: True when this individual is the designated control person per CDD (one is required when no owner crosses 25%).

schema.name: BusinessLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent Business.

schema.name: Meets25PercentThreshold
schema.Description: True when OwnershipPercentage >= 25.

schema.name: MeetsCddThreshold
schema.Description: True when this row counts toward CDD compliance: meets 25% OR is the control person.

Contacts.Description: Non-owner individuals associated with a Business: officers, AP clerks, authorized signers, additional points of contact. Distinct from BeneficialOwners (which carries PII/CDD weight).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased compound PK: {Business}-{FullName slug}-{Title slug}.

schema.name: Business
schema.Description: FK to the parent Business.

schema.name: FullName
schema.Description: Contact's full name.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Job title at the business (CEO, CFO, AP Clerk, etc.).

schema.name: Email
schema.Description: Contact email.

schema.name: Phone
schema.Description: Contact phone.

schema.name: ContactType
schema.Description: Discriminator: Officer, APClerk, AuthorizedSigner, Other.

schema.name: IsAuthorizedSigner
schema.Description: True when this contact can sign on the business's accounts.

schema.name: BusinessLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent Business.

schema.name: IsOfficer
schema.Description: True when ContactType = 'Officer'.

schema.name: IsApClerk
schema.Description: True when ContactType = 'APClerk'.

Accounts.Description: Deposit accounts the Business holds at the bank (checking, savings, money market). Balances feed GlobalCashFlow during credit analysis. TreasuryServices enrollment flags (ACH, Wire, Card) are stored per-account.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased compound PK: {Business}-{AccountType}-{Last4}.

schema.name: Business
schema.Description: FK to the parent Business.

schema.name: AccountType
schema.Description: Checking, Savings, MoneyMarket, etc.

schema.name: AccountNumberLast4
schema.Description: Last 4 digits of the account number for display.

schema.name: CurrentBalanceUsd
schema.Description: Current balance in USD; sourced from CoreBanking.

schema.name: HasAch
schema.Description: ACH treasury service enrolled on this account.

schema.name: HasWire
schema.Description: Wire transfer treasury service enrolled on this account.

schema.name: HasCard
schema.Description: Business card product issued against this account.

schema.name: OpenedAt
schema.Description: Date the account was opened.

schema.name: BusinessLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent Business.

schema.name: TreasuryServiceCount
schema.Description: Number of treasury services enrolled on this account.

schema.name: HasAnyTreasuryService
schema.Description: True when at least one treasury service is enrolled.

Loans.Description: Credit facilities extended to a Business, tracked from inquiry through funding, servicing, and payoff. RiskRating is denormalized here for fast reads; every change is captured in RiskRatingHistory.
Loans.summary_rich: The **credit book** — every facility the bank has on the borrower. A Loan carries the live denormalized RiskRating (with the full migration history kept in `RiskRatingHistory` so regulators can audit drift), the DSCR and LTV bands, and the segregation-of-duties check that the originating RM cannot also be the underwriter. `OnWatchlist` is the union the credit committee actually sorts by: any loan that's classified (grade ≥ 7) *or* has a covenant in breach.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from LoanNumber (e.g. 'L-0051' -> 'l-0051').

schema.name: LoanNumber
schema.Description: Bank-assigned loan number (e.g. 'L-0051').

schema.name: Business
schema.Description: FK to the borrower Business.

schema.name: OriginatingRm
schema.Description: FK to Users: the RM who originated this loan.

schema.name: Underwriter
schema.Description: FK to Users: the underwriter who underwrote this loan.

schema.name: LoanPurpose
schema.Description: Equipment, CRE, LOC, WorkingCapital, etc.

schema.name: PrincipalUsd
schema.Description: Loan principal at origination, USD.

schema.name: RatePct
schema.Description: Interest rate, percent (e.g. 7.25).

schema.name: TermMonths
schema.Description: Loan term in months.

schema.name: UnderwritingStage
schema.Description: Current pipeline stage: Inquiry, PreQualification, Application, KYBAndBureauPull, Packaging, CreditAnalysis, CommitteeApproval, ClosingPrep, Signing, Funded.

schema.name: RiskRating
schema.Description: Current risk grade (1=Pass strongest ... 7=Substandard ... 9=Loss). Denormalized; every change is captured in RiskRatingHistory.

schema.name: RiskRatingLabel
schema.Description: Human-readable label for the current risk grade.

schema.name: Dscr
schema.Description: Most recent debt service coverage ratio.

schema.name: Ltv
schema.Description: Most recent loan-to-value ratio (0-1); null for unsecured loans.

schema.name: GlobalCashFlowUsd
schema.Description: Most recent global cash flow figure across business, owners, and affiliates.

schema.name: OriginatedAt
schema.Description: Date the application was created.

schema.name: FundedAt
schema.Description: Date the loan funded; null until funding completes.

schema.name: BusinessLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the borrower Business.

schema.name: BusinessNaicsCode
schema.Description: NAICS code of the borrower Business (concentration analytics).

schema.name: OriginatingRmLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the originating RM.

schema.name: UnderwriterLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the underwriter.

schema.name: IsFunded
schema.Description: True when UnderwritingStage = 'Funded'.

schema.name: IsClassifiedAsset
schema.Description: True when RiskRating is substandard (7) or worse.

schema.name: DscrInBand
schema.Description: True when DSCR meets or exceeds the 1.20 minimum band.

schema.name: LtvInBand
schema.Description: True when LTV is at or below 0.80, or null (unsecured).

schema.name: SegregationOfDutiesOk
schema.Description: True when the originating RM and the underwriter are different Users (segregation-of-duties check).
schema.explanation_rich: **An auditable control, encoded as a calc.** Bank policy (and bank examiners) require that the person who originates a loan cannot also be the person who underwrites it — one person on each side of the credit decision. This field is the live check. Worked example: *L-0051 (Stillwater Roasters)* lists Devon Marshall (RM) as originator and Priya Iyer (Underwriter) as underwriter — two distinct Users, so the flag is TRUE. Reassign the underwriter to Devon and the same field flips to FALSE the same instant. Because every substrate reads `SegregationOfDutiesOk` from the same definition, there is no app-side guard to forget — the control travels with the data.

schema.name: CountOfCovenants
schema.Description: Number of Covenants attached to this loan.

schema.name: CountOfBreachedCovenants
schema.Description: Number of Covenants currently in Breached status on this loan.

schema.name: CountOfRiskRatingHistory
schema.Description: Number of RiskRatingHistory rows for this loan.

schema.name: CountOfDocuments
schema.Description: Number of Documents attached directly to this Loan.

schema.name: HasBreachedCovenant
schema.Description: True when at least one covenant is in breach.

schema.name: OnWatchlist
schema.Description: Higher-order: on the watchlist when the loan is a classified asset OR has any breached covenant.
schema.explanation_rich: **The watchlist is a union of two independent signals.** A loan lands on the credit committee's watchlist if *either* its risk grade has slipped into classified territory (RiskRating ≥ 7) *or* one of its covenants is currently in breach. Worked example: *L-0042 (Pine Hardware CRE refi)* satisfies **both** — RiskRating=7 (Substandard) makes `IsClassifiedAsset` TRUE, and its MinDSCR covenant is in Breached status making `HasBreachedCovenant` TRUE — so `OnWatchlist` = TRUE. *L-0051 (Stillwater equipment loan)* is grade 4 with all covenants Active — `OnWatchlist` = FALSE. Cure the DSCR covenant on L-0042 *and* upgrade the grade back below 7, and the same field falls off the watchlist in every substrate at once.

schema.name: HealthScore
schema.Description: Higher-order composite health score (0-4): +1 for DscrInBand, +1 for LtvInBand, +1 for NOT IsClassifiedAsset, +1 for NOT HasBreachedCovenant.

Covenants.Description: Conditions attached to a Loan that must be tested on a recurring schedule (e.g. minimum DSCR each quarter). CovenantMonitoring runs the tickler calendar ahead of NextTestDate; breaches surface as SystemEvent Interactions.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased compound PK: {Loan}-{CovenantType slug}.

schema.name: Loan
schema.Description: FK to the parent Loan.

schema.name: CovenantType
schema.Description: MinDSCR, MaxLeverage, ReportingFinancials, etc.

schema.name: ThresholdValue
schema.Description: Numeric threshold for the test (e.g. 1.20 for MinDSCR); null for non-numeric covenants.

schema.name: TestFrequency
schema.Description: Quarterly, Annual, Monthly, etc.

schema.name: NextTestDate
schema.Description: Date of the next scheduled covenant test.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Active, Breached, Waived, Cured, Closed.

schema.name: CurrentWaiverThrough
schema.Description: Date through which an active CovenantWaiver is in effect; null when no active waiver.

schema.name: LoanLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent Loan.

schema.name: LoanBusiness
schema.Description: Business of the parent Loan (chained lookup).

schema.name: IsBreached
schema.Description: True when Status = 'Breached'.

schema.name: HasActiveWaiver
schema.Description: True when CurrentWaiverThrough is set (waiver is on file).

RiskRatingHistory.Description: Time-series of risk-grade changes on a Loan. Regulators audit rating drift, so every migration is captured here. AnnualReview also writes a row even when the grade is reaffirmed unchanged.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased compound PK: {Loan}-{EffectiveDate}-grade-{NewGrade}.

schema.name: Loan
schema.Description: FK to the Loan whose grade changed.

schema.name: ChangedByUser
schema.Description: FK to Users: who recorded the migration.

schema.name: EffectiveDate
schema.Description: Date the new grade takes effect.

schema.name: PriorGrade
schema.Description: Grade before this change; null on initial rating.

schema.name: NewGrade
schema.Description: Grade after this change.

schema.name: Reason
schema.Description: Free-text justification for the migration.

schema.name: LoanLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent Loan.

schema.name: ChangedByUserLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the user who recorded the change.

schema.name: GradeDelta
schema.Description: NewGrade - PriorGrade (positive = downgrade in this scale); equals 0 on initial rating.

schema.name: IsDowngrade
schema.Description: True when the migration represents a downgrade (higher grade number = worse risk).

schema.name: IsInitialRating
schema.Description: True when this is the loan's first rating row (no PriorGrade).

schema.name: CrossedClassifiedThreshold
schema.Description: Higher-order: true when this migration is the moment a loan crossed from non-classified to classified (PriorGrade < 7 and NewGrade >= 7).
schema.explanation_rich: **The bitemporal moment of becoming a classified asset.** Regulators don't just care that a loan *is* classified today — they care *when* it crossed, because that's the clock for ALLL reserves, watchlist reporting, and committee escalation. This calc fires TRUE on exactly the one history row where the loan stepped over the line. Worked example: *L-0042 (Pine Hardware)* has three rating rows — 2021-05-20 initial (Prior=null, New=4), 2025-05-02 downgrade (Prior=4, New=6), and **2025-08-15 downgrade (Prior=6, New=7)** — that last row, and only that row, evaluates `CrossedClassifiedThreshold = TRUE`. The 2025-05-02 row is also a downgrade (`IsDowngrade = TRUE`) but stayed inside Pass/Special-Mention so this flag is FALSE there. Because every grade change is captured in `RiskRatingHistory`, the rulebook can be replayed at any historical point and the answer is the same.

data.Name: l-0042-2025-05-02-grade-6
data.Reason: DSCR covenant breach; downgrade to Special Mention.

data.Name: l-0042-2025-08-15-grade-7
data.Reason: Sustained underperformance; downgrade to Substandard. Added to Watchlist.

data.Name: l-0078-2024-08-22-grade-3
data.Reason: Initial rating at funding; strong cash flow coverage.

Documents.Description: Files in the DocumentVault. A document attaches to either a Business (tax returns, formation docs) or a Loan (note, security agreement, appraisal). Both FKs are nullable so a document can hang off the appropriate parent.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the filename.

schema.name: Business
schema.Description: Optional FK to a Business (nullable for loan-only docs).

schema.name: Loan
schema.Description: Optional FK to a Loan (nullable for business-only docs).

schema.name: UploadedByUser
schema.Description: Optional FK to the User who uploaded (null when uploaded via the BusinessClientPortal).

schema.name: Filename
schema.Description: Original filename in the vault.

schema.name: DocumentType
schema.Description: TaxReturn, FormationDoc, FinancialStatement, Note, SecurityAgreement, Appraisal, etc.

schema.name: OcrIndexed
schema.Description: True once OCR has indexed the document into SearchAndIndexing.

schema.name: UploadedVia
schema.Description: Source surface: RMDashboard, BranchBankerPortal, BusinessClientPortal, AdminConsole, API.

schema.name: UploadedAt
schema.Description: Upload timestamp.

schema.name: BusinessLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the attached Business (if any).

schema.name: LoanLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the attached Loan (if any).

schema.name: UploadedByUserLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the uploader (if known).

schema.name: AttachedTo
schema.Description: Which parent this document hangs off: 'Loan', 'Business', or 'Orphan' (neither set).

schema.name: FromCustomerPortal
schema.Description: True when uploaded by the customer themselves via the BusinessClientPortal.

Interactions.Description: Unified activity-log feed for a Business. An Interaction is intentionally generic: InteractionType discriminates Note, Call, Visit, Task, Meeting, or SystemEvent. Covenant breaches, document requests, and other machine actions are written as SystemEvent interactions so they appear in the same stream as human-logged activity.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased compound PK: {Business}-{InteractionDate}-{InteractionType slug}-{Subject slug}.

schema.name: Business
schema.Description: FK to the parent Business.

schema.name: User
schema.Description: FK to Users: who logged the interaction (null for system-generated or customer-portal-originated rows).

schema.name: InteractionType
schema.Description: Note, Call, Visit, Task, Meeting, or SystemEvent.

schema.name: Subject
schema.Description: Short subject line (used in compound PK).

schema.name: Body
schema.Description: Long-form body of the interaction.

schema.name: InteractionDate
schema.Description: Date the interaction occurred or was logged.

schema.name: DueDate
schema.Description: For Task-type interactions: due date.

schema.name: Source
schema.Description: Origin surface: RMDashboard, BusinessClientPortal, BranchBankerPortal, SystemEvent.

schema.name: BusinessLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent Business.

schema.name: UserLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the logging user (if any).

schema.name: IsSystemEvent
schema.Description: True for system-generated rows.

schema.name: IsTask
schema.Description: True for Task-type rows.

schema.name: FromCustomer
schema.Description: True when sourced from the BusinessClientPortal (customer-originated).

schema.name: IsCovenantEvent
schema.Description: Higher-order: true when this is a covenant-related system event (subject contains 'covenant').

__meta__.Description: Project-level metadata that travels with the rulebook: tagline, motif, narrative descriptions, substrate list, signature rows, etc. One row per metadata key. Use ValueType to interpret StringValue vs JsonValue.

schema.name: MetaKey
schema.Description: The metadata key (e.g. 'tagline', 'motif_palette', 'substrates'). Unique within the table.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Identifier for this metadata entry. Mirrors MetaKey so the row is addressable by Name like every other table.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: How to interpret the value columns: 'string' (use StringValue), 'object' (parse JsonValue as JSON object), 'array' (parse JsonValue as JSON array).

schema.name: StringValue
schema.Description: Plain string value. Populated when ValueType == 'string'; null otherwise.

schema.name: JsonValue
schema.Description: JSON-encoded value. Populated when ValueType == 'object' or 'array'; null when ValueType == 'string'.

data.Name: tagline
data.StringValue: A community bank's commercial book — loans, covenants, CDD, and the watchlist.

data.Name: description_rich
data.StringValue: Effortless Banking models a community bank's commercial relationship-management desk — five small-business borrowers, five bank employees, three funded loans, the covenants attached to them, the CDD beneficial-ownership file, and the activity stream the regulators eventually audit. The interesting thing isn't the data, it's that **every regulated rule lives in the rulebook**: the FinCEN 25%-or-control-person CDD test, the substandard-asset threshold (RiskRating ≥ 7), the watchlist union of *classified OR breached covenant*, the segregation-of-duties check that the originating RM cannot also be the underwriter — all of it is calculated fields wired into a DAG. The same answer Postgres returns to the credit committee is the same answer the Python conformance tests assert. There is no second copy of the rules hiding in application code.

data.Name: use_cases
data.JsonValue: ["**Watch a loan cross the classified-asset line.** Open *L-0042 (Pine Hardware)*'s `RiskRatingHistory`: the 2025-08-15 row carries PriorGrade=6, NewGrade=7, and `CrossedClassifiedThreshold` flips to TRUE — the moment the loan becomes a regulator-reportable classified asset.", "**Confirm CDD on a new prospect.** Hilltop Bakery is a Prospect with no BeneficialOwners attached yet — `MeetsCddRule` is FALSE and `PortfolioPriority` reads 'Medium'. Add owners totalling ≥25% (or a designated control person) and the rule clears the same instant the rulebook recomputes.", "**Enforce segregation of duties.** Try to set L-0051's Underwriter to the same user as its OriginatingRM (Devon Marshall) and `SegregationOfDutiesOk` falls to FALSE. The same flag is exposed identically in Postgres and Python — no app-side guard to forget.", "**Trace a covenant breach into the activity feed.** L-0042's MinDSCR covenant is in Breached status; the matching SystemEvent Interaction on 2025-05-02 lives in the same stream as human-logged calls and visits, so the audit trail and the RM's daily feed are one table, not two.", "**Read the watchlist in two substrates.** Ask *'which loans are on the watchlist?'* of the Postgres view, then of the Python module — both return L-0042 because `OnWatchlist` is the same calculated field on both sides."]

data.Name: journal_seed
data.StringValue: Three funded loans, one classified — L-0042 (Pine Hardware) crossed into Substandard in August and its DSCR covenant is in breach. The rest of the book is clean; Hilltop Bakery is one CDD file away from becoming a customer.

data.Name: CMCC_Summary
data.StringValue: Authored-direct ERB rulebook for a community-bank commercial relationship management platform. No Airtable round-trip; this rulebook is the SSoT at the top of the Leopold loop. Targets Postgres via effortless build.

data.Name: conversion_metadata
data.JsonValue: {"source": "authored-from-narrative", "table_count": 10, "tool_version": "shadle-bootstrap-1.0", "ssot": "effortless-rulebook.json", "next_step": "effortless build -> generated Postgres schema and vw_* views"}
